Name: Arabic Unicode name
Description: Derivation of Arabic code points based on Unicode name

Name: Case derivation
Description: General assumption that the lowercase form derives from the uppercase
Source: UCD # UnicodeData.txt simple upper & lower case mapping properties

Name: Compound Egyptian Hieroglyphs
Description: Hieroglyph ligatures composed from base hieroglyphs
Notes: This process reads Hieroglyph references in text descriptions. This can result in derivation chains being inaccurately portrayed if only base Hieroglyphs are mentioned. Eg. a derivation chain for hieroglyph C such as (A->B; B->C) could render as (A->C; B->C) depending on the descriptions.

Name: Cuneiform Unicode name
Description: Derivation of Cuneiform code points based on Unicode name
Notes: For atomic signs, Cuneiform has no known parent script (Proto-Cuneiform not in DB or Unicode yet)

Name: Equivalencies from name
Description: Derivations based on equivalencies inferred from Unicode name

Name: Ge'ez Unicode name
Description: Derivation of Ge'ez code points by identifying the inherent vowel parent based on Unicode name
Source: Wikipedia: Geʽez script#Geʽez_abugida/UCD # UnicodeData.txt name property

Name: Indic letters
Description: Indic derivations from cognate letters and known script ancestors

Name: Latin Unicode name
Description: Derivation of Latin code points based on Unicode name

Name: Independent scripts - general
Description: Sets characters from independent scripts to independently derived
Notes: General assumption for independent scripts; could be incorrect due to script-internal derivations (mitigated for some scripts)

Name: Semitic letters
Description: Semitic derivations from cognate letters and known script ancestors

Name: Simplified Chinese
Description: Derivation of Simplified Chinese code points from their corresponding Traditional Chinese code points

Name: Sogdian Unicode name
Description: Derivation of Sogdian code points based on matching Old Sogdian Unicode names

Name: Supplementary Indic
Description: Indic derivations inferred by Unicode name and known script ancestors

Name: Tangut radicals
Description: Set Tangut radicals and components to independently derived
Notes: Bare Tangut radicals/components are largely a modern scholarly invention; the Tanguts are not known to have deconstructed their characters systematically to the unit level (and the few that do exist independently were not encoded as radicals in Unicode). However, it would be unhelpful to mark them as descending from perhaps hundreds of characters, so this database treats them as having been independently invented by the Tanguts.
